articles.title: ‘Khan Sir Won't Surrender In Patna Court, To File Anticipatory Bail Plea On 8 June’: Lawyer
articles.description: Educationist and coaching institute owner Faisal Khan, widely known as Khan Sir, will not surrender before the Patna Civil Court in connection with a case linked to the June 2 attack on his coaching centre, according to his lawyer Arvind Kumar Mavvar. The legal team plans to seek anticipatory bail, with the lawyer stating that the deadline to file the plea ended on Saturday and that the application would now be moved on Monday. The case relates to an attack on Khan Sir’s institute, where videos showed several individuals allegedly assaulting a security guard, pelting bricks and stones, and tearing down posters outside the premises. While reports initially suggested firing, Patna Police denied any gunfire. Two security guards employed at the institute have already been arrested and sent to jail, while Khan Sir was questioned during the investigation.

articles.title: At 92, woman with severe heart valve disease finds relief through minimally invasive procedure
articles.description: A 92-year-old woman, deemed too frail for open-heart surgery due to severe heart failure, has regained her mobility and confidence after a groundbreaking minimally invasive procedure. Doctors successfully repaired her leaky mitral valve using a MitraClip device, significantly improving her breathing and quality of life. This innovative treatment offers new hope for high-risk cardiac patients.

articles.title: PM Modi shares video on Make in India war machines: Atmanirbhar Bharat campaign

Sharing a video after the visit, Modi said India reiterated its commitment to becoming “strong and self-reliant” as he launched projects worth around Rs 18,800 crore in Surat.

articles.title: Why Gujarat’s ‘single-window’ GCAS admission system is facing growing criticism
articles.description: Gujarat's ambitious single-window admission system, GCAS, faces criticism for adding complexity. Students now navigate multiple registrations, entrance exams, and counselling rounds, leading to confusion and operational hurdles. Despite promises of a unified process, the reality involves numerous steps, prompting questions about its effectiveness and causing seat vacancies.

articles.title: A pet cat naps on Lord Krishna idol’s bed, and the internet can’t stop smiling
articles.description: A heartwarming video of Rehant Thakur conversing with his cat, Ketu, sleeping on Lord Krishna's bed has charmed the internet. Rehant playfully asks Ketu whose bed it is, but the unbothered feline remains asleep. Viewers adored the gentle interaction and Ketu's relaxed demeanor, calling it wholesome content that has captured many hearts.

articles.title: E85 fuel to get Rs 20/litre cheaper as India ramps up ethanol push
articles.description: India is launching E85 petrol, an 85% ethanol blend, at discounted prices. This move aims to boost clean fuel adoption and cut reliance on imported fossil fuels. The government plans a phased rollout of E85 dispensing stations nationwide. Vehicle manufacturers are introducing E85 compatible models. This initiative is expected to benefit farmers and save foreign exchange.

articles.title: US-Iran war LIVE: CENTCOM says it shot down Iranian drones launched towards Hormuz, stuck coastal radar sites
articles.description: US-Iran war LIVE: US Central Command said it hit the radar sites, including an island in the Strait of Hormuz, "to defend against further attacks." It was the latest in a back-and-forth of attacks that have strained the tenuous ceasefire in the war and efforts to reach a deal to extend it.

articles.title: SC: Can’t use OSA to deny accused access to documents relied on by prosecution
articles.description: The Supreme Court has ruled that an accused's right to a fair trial includes access to prosecution documents, even if classified under the Official Secrets Act. The court ordered the government to provide highly confidential documents to retired Major General V.K. Singh, facing charges for revealing secrets in his book, emphasizing fair trial rights.

articles.title: McDonald's Drive-Thru AI Upgrade: How Google Is Replacing the Failed IBM Ordering System
articles.description: McDonald's has announced plans to roll out a new Google-powered AI operating system called ArchIQ across all US restaurants, featuring a drive-thru assistant named Archy that can handle orders in English and Spanish. The move comes after the chain scrapped its previous IBM-built AI ordering system in 2024 following widespread complaints about incorrect orders.
